title: Phase 3: Close the write-back loop + capture surface polish
summary: Make Claude Code sessions feed the brain, polish the PWA capture screen and introduce the async write path that lets the iOS Shortcut surface return in <100ms, and ship the iOS voice Shortcut itself.
outOfScope: New entity types beyond what migration produced (Phase 4 Track D, demand-driven)
Apple Watch complication and PWA dark mode polish (Phase 4 Track F)
technicalConsiderations: New templates/claude-code-client/write_session_episode.py registered on SessionEnd; lands in any wired-up client repo's .claude/hooks/

In-process FIFO write queue inside the PWA Node process — no persistent spool. Acceptable failure mode for a single-user system: PWA crash mid-drain loses captured-but-not-yet-extracted items. Add a JSON-lines spool only if data loss becomes a real complaint

POST /api/capture changes from synchronous (Phase 2) to enqueue + 202 (Phase 3) so the iOS Shortcut surface can return in <100ms; the chat-side add_episode tool stays synchronous so agent responses can reflect the new episode in the same turn
iOS Shortcut config doc lives in pwa/docs/ios-shortcut.md
Risks: in-memory queue is a SPOF (mitigation: graceful-shutdown drain), iOS Shortcut bearer-at-rest, summary verbosity tuning
